description_long: This is a rounded, tripod-footed ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a bulbous body. The surface is adorned with intricate, hand-painted motifs in earthy tones, including stylized human faces, geometric shapes, and abstract patterns reminiscent of Mesoamerican or Pre-Columbian art. The vase's exterior shows signs of age, with a matte, slightly rough finish and some visible discoloration and wear. The three short, sturdy legs elevate the vessel, giving it a distinctive, stable stance. The interior appears unglazed and is darkened, likely from use or age. Overall, the vase exudes a sense of historical craftsmanship and cultural significance.
description: A tripod-footed, bulbous ceramic vase with hand-painted Mesoamerican-style motifs and a wide, flared mouth. The surface is matte, aged, and decorated with stylized faces and abstract patterns.
description_view_0: This view highlights a prominent painted human face with elaborate headdress and geometric motifs, as well as two of the three supporting legs.
description_view_1: This angle shows more abstract and geometric painted designs, with less emphasis on figurative elements and all three legs visible.
description_view_2: Here, another stylized face is visible, along with additional decorative patterns and the darkened interior of the vase.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the side with flowing, abstract motifs and the curvature of the vase, with one leg most prominent.
